preamble: The reduction of the voiceless dental fricative [θ] to a neutral schwa [ə] or its complete elision is a common feature of rapid speech, particularly in unstressed syllables or complex consonant clusters.

examples.note: Reduction of the complex cluster by replacing the dental fricative with a reduced vocalic segment or schwa-like off-glide.
Historical reduction of dental fricatives before complete loss or replacement.
